title: Oscars 2026: Ryan Coogler Wins Best Original Screenplay for Sinners
text: Ryan Coogler claimed his first Academy Award at the 98th Oscars ceremony, winning Best Original Screenplay for the vampire thriller "Sinners" starring Michael B. Jordan. The Win: The Warner Bros. film entered this year's competition with a record 16 nominations, making it the most nominated film in Academy Awards history. Coogler's script victory marks a significant milestone in his career, which previously included Oscar nominations as a producer for "Judas and the Black Messiah" and recognition for the song "Lift Me Up" from "Black Panther: Wakanda Forever." Coogler's Acceptance Speech: Receiving a standing ovation, Coogler began emotionally: "Please, please, please sit down, 'cause I'm very nervous and they're gonna play me off." He expressed gratitude to the Academy, fellow nominees, and his collaborators, calling them "amazing." The filmmaker extended particular appreciation to his family, including his wife Zinzi Evans and three children. He reflected on their relationship: "Every day I get to spend with you is better than the one that came before." Career Impact: Before this Oscar win, Coogler had established himself through work on "Creed" and the "Black Panther" franchise. The "Sinners" screenplay victory continues the film's remarkable awards-season momentum.
